primary_topics: Allegheny Steel and Dick Simmons (CEO who avoided unprofitable quarters in 1980s)
Aluminum history: Ørsted (1825), Napoleonic rattle, Washington Monument, Hall–Héroult patent race (1886)
Why Wall Street loved the aluminum price drop but hated the steel productivity gain — new vs. old markets, capacity dynamics
Turbine blade coatings (Ni-Ti bond coat, zirconia thermal barrier) and Teflon-to-ceramic cookware analogy
Optical glass progression — Egyptian → Phoenician → 1900 eyeglass → Corning optical fiber
Harvard Glass Flowers (Blaschka) — digression on glass science and long-term stability
Copper industry crisis 1970s — bandwidth limits, IBM copper-on-silicon (~1990), tungsten barrier layer
Pentium chip clock-speed and inter-die distance limits
Penny composition: copper → zinc (early 1980s); melt-value economics
High-temperature superconductors 1989 — NSF funding stampede, Tom's skepticism, pinning-center problem
Eagar's 1975 doctoral thesis on superconductivity (first word-processed thesis in DMSE)
Limits to materials — Ashby plots, Lennard-Jones bonding potential, modulus as ∂²E/∂R²
Composites — X-33 space plane, V-22 Osprey, 777/787; cheap composites still elusive
Concrete and asphalt as the high-volume composites; transportation cost dominates low-value materials
cases_mentioned: X-33 space plane composite hydrogen tanks ($12,000/lb)
